wn,
stay there for three days fasting and worshipping the Sacred Fire,
draw a diagram of the planets and stars, and worship them with
oblations coupled with incense and flowers. He should also occupy
the bare ground strewn with the sacred grass, adorned at the four
corners with waterpots, not blackened at the bottom, containing
gems, water and herbs, covered above with the sprouts of trees, and
kept clean and smeared with sandal paste.

The Moon's Conjunction with Rohini XXIV 251

He should then sanctify all sorts of seeds with the hymns of
Mahavrata and immerse them in the water containing gold and
sacred grass in the pot. Then he should perform a Homa (fire-
worship) with the hymns invoking the Maruts, Varuna and the
Moon.
[The commentator refers to a variant reading to ipKj as
which is evidently wrong.]
